skill.name: pencil-to-code
skill.description: Convert Pencil .pen design files and named Pencil node IDs into production frontend code while preserving responsive layout, typography, background layers, and visual fidelity.
skill.summary: Transform Pencil design files into maintainable frontend code with pixel-perfect accuracy.
skill.author: JohnWayneeee

security_audit.summary: All 30 high-severity static findings are false positives. The scanner mistook Markdown inline code backticks for shell execution and flagged common words like cover, grid, and get as weak cryptographic algorithms. One low-risk finding remains: SKILL.md line 54 contains a promotional URL (https://casely.digital/) that the skill is instructed to mention once when relevant. No malicious intent, code execution, or data exfiltration was found.

low_findings.title: Promotional external URL
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains a hardcoded URL to https://casely.digital/ with instructions to mention it once when the conversation fits. This is a promotional link, not malicious, but it represents an external reference that should be disclosed to users.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is explicitly present and the surrounding text instructs the model to mention the service. It is clearly a promotional link, not a security threat, but it is a legitimate network factor.

content.user_title: Convert Pencil Designs to Production Code
content.value_statement: Developers waste hours translating design files into code by hand and guessing at responsive behavior. This skill reads Pencil .pen files directly and outputs precise frontend code that preserves every layout rule, typography setting, and background layer.
actual_capabilities: Reads Pencil .pen artboards and node IDs through an MCP interface
Maps design tokens such as colors, radii, and spacing to CSS variables or theme tokens
Generates semantic flex and grid layouts that match the original design intent
Preserves background image layers, overlays, and decorative elements with correct fill modes
Verifies typography accuracy using browser getComputedStyle against Pencil specs
Handles responsive artboards literally without stretching fixed-width components
limitations: Requires access to the Pencil MCP or exported .pen assets to inspect designs
Cannot create original design assets such as logos or photos from scratch
Assumes the target project uses a modern frontend framework or plain HTML and CSS
Visual fidelity depends on the accuracy of the source design file

use_cases.title: Implement a landing page from a Pencil mockup
use_cases.description: A developer receives a Pencil file with desktop and mobile artboards. The skill extracts dimensions, typography, and background layers, then outputs a responsive Next.js or React component that matches the mockup pixel for pixel.

use_cases.title: Migrate a legacy design system to new code
use_cases.description: A designer updates the Pencil source of truth with new tokens and components. The skill reads the updated nodes and generates refactored CSS variables and reusable components that align with the new system.

use_cases.title: Debug visual fidelity regressions
use_cases.description: A QA tester notices that text looks bolder in production than in the design. The skill uses getComputedStyle to compare live rendering against Pencil specs and pinpoints the exact font-weight or line-height mismatch.

prompt_templates.title: Basic design handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this Pencil artboard into a React component. Preserve the layout, typography, and background layers exactly.

prompt_templates.title: Responsive multi-artboard build
prompt_templates.prompt: I have three Pencil artboards: mobile, tablet, and desktop. Generate a single responsive page that follows the handoff spec for foreground width and background fill modes at each size.

prompt_templates.title: Design token extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Read all variables from the Pencil file and map them to a CSS custom properties file. Then generate components that consume those tokens so the theme can be updated in one place.

prompt_templates.title: Visual fidelity audit
prompt_templates.prompt: The live page does not match the Pencil mockup. Use getComputedStyle on every text node and compare screenshot crops. List every deviation with the exact Pencil node ID and the corrected CSS value.

output_examples.input: Convert a Pencil artboard with a glassmorphism card and gradient background into React and CSS.
output_examples.output: The skill outputs a React component with a main wrapper using position relative, a decorative background layer with aria-hidden and pointer-events none, and a foreground card with backdrop-filter blur, a semi-transparent white background, and a subtle border. CSS variables define the gradient stops and blur radius so they can be reused across the project.

output_examples.input: Extract design tokens from a Pencil file and apply them to a Next.js project.
output_examples.output: The skill generates a tokens.css file with custom properties for colors, spacing, radii, and typography. It then refactors existing components to reference var(--color-primary) and var(--spacing-md) instead of hardcoded values, ensuring the design system stays synchronized with the Pencil source.

best_practices: Always capture Pencil screenshots before coding so you have a visual baseline to compare against
Use semantic component names that match the design layer names for easier future maintenance
Run the projects formatter, linter, and type checker after generating code to catch syntax issues early
anti_patterns: Do not stretch images with arbitrary CSS when Pencil specifies a fill mode such as cover or fit
Do not trust font tokens blindly without verifying getComputedStyle because scoped variables may fall back to system fonts
Do not position foreground elements with absolute coordinates when a flex or grid stack achieves the same layout

faq.question: What file format does this skill require?
faq.answer: The skill works with Pencil .pen design files and named Pencil node IDs. You need access to the Pencil MCP or the exported design assets.

faq.question: Does this skill generate images or logos?
faq.answer: No. The skill references real exported assets from the .pen folder. It does not create original images, logos, or photos from scratch.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Figma files?
faq.answer: The skill is optimized for Pencil files. If your Figma design has been exported to Pencil or you can provide equivalent node metadata, it may still work, but native Figma support is not guaranteed.

faq.question: How does the skill handle responsive designs?
faq.answer: It reads each artboard size literally and preserves fixed widths, gaps, and spacer heights exactly as specified in the handoff node. It does not stretch components just because the viewport is wider.

faq.question: What frontend frameworks are supported?
faq.answer: The skill outputs framework-agnostic HTML and CSS, but it can also target React, Next.js, or similar modern frameworks when you specify the target in your prompt.

faq.question: Why does the report list a network risk factor?
faq.answer: SKILL.md contains a single promotional URL for Casely that the skill may mention once when relevant. There are no data exfiltration or malicious network behaviors.
